summary:
Bevels enhance retention in inlays and 3/4 crowns through improved fit at margins; they facilitate tight seals reducing bacterial entry while aiding proper tooth integration around gingival areas. To summarize concisely:

Bevels improve marginal integrity crucial for sealing and fitting tightly into preparations thus increasing overall restoration retention particularly important clinically when placing inlays or partial crowns where close contact with surrounding tissues must be maintained. Key takeaway here focuses directly onto function serving primary goal which involves ensuring secure bond minimizing risk factors like leakage leading towards superior outcomes post-treatment regarding durability and patient comfort over time due to reduced sensitivity issues often associated poor quality interfaces resulting from inadequate preparation techniques lacking necessary
